page: Microsoft Azure F4als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Falsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at 3.7 GHz. The system is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier (top 10%) and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Multi-core performance for both stress-ng and Geekbench falls in the average range. Memory performance presents a clear tradeoff: while cached small-block read bandwidth is average, uncached large-block read bandwidth and PassMark memory scores are weak, falling into the bottom 25%.

Given its high clock speed and dedicated CPU allocation paired with a low memory footprint, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound tasks that do not require large memory capacities. It is well-suited for single-threaded processing, lightweight web serving, and basic compilation tasks, but less optimal for memory-intensive databases or large-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F4als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and vCPUs, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM, representing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core results in the top 10% and strong single-threaded decompression and Geekbench scores. However, multi-core performance is average, and uncached large-block memory read bandwidth is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy, single-threaded workloads, software compilation, and lightweight web serving, but is constrained for memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F4als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core CPU performance for compute-heavy workloads.

tagline: A compute-focused virtual machine delivering top-tier single-core performance with dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a lean memory profile.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in the top 10%
No local storage or integrated GPU included
Best suited for single-threaded compute tasks and light web serving
